name: Charles Tolliver
abstract: Charles Tolliver (born March 6, 1942) is an American jazz trumpeter, composer, and co-founder of Strata East Records.

interviews.title: Charles Tolliver interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2021 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: So Charles and I would play sessions together... Tolliver said, 'Listen man, you know, I'm gonna tell Jackie about you... Jackie called me and said 'Yeah, Charles Tolliver told me about you. I want you to play in the band.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWEE] I recorded with him too, huh? ... Charles Tolliver on trumpet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Harrison
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Sun Ra with Charles Tolliver too and we played other gigs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Harrison interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 17, 2022 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: From that, Stanley Cowell and Charles Tolliver started a record company... Charles and Stanley talked to the people in Detroit... and they started Strata-East in New York, the record company.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: While I appreciated performing the music of Charles Tolliver... I still wanted to be spending time with something, some entity that would be performing my own music
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Russell
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions Charles Tolliver as co-owner of Strata-East label; INTERVIEWEE responds 'That’s interesting.' with no indication of personal interaction.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Russell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
